content.full_text:
John Glenn
36601 Cuenca Court
Fremont, California 94536

April 14, 1992


Scott Hallabrin, Acting General Counsel
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P O Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

RE:  Request for Advice Regarding Conflict of Interest

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

     I am an elected member of the Board of Directors of the
San Francisco Bay Area Rapid Transit District ("BART"). BART is
in the process of constructing extensions to the existing BART
system, one of which includes an extension south of BART's Daly
City station in San Mateo County in the vicinity of the San
Francisco International Airport.

     I own an insurance adjusting business known as John
Glenn Adjusters and Administrators. During the past twelve
months my adjusting business has received more than $250 in
income for insurance adjusting services under a contract with the
San Mateo County Transit District ("SamTrans"). My business has
performed claims adjusting services for SamTrans for
approximately ten years and the business is paid for those
services directly by SamTrans.

     After several years of negotiations, in February 1990
BART and SamTrans entered into a "Comprehensive Agreement Per-
taining to BART System Extensions." In general this agreement
provides for a means of extending the BART system to the vicinity
of the San Francisco International Airport in San Mateo County.
Under the terms of the agreement BART is obliged to apply to the
Federal Transit Authority ("FTA"), formerly the Urban Mass
Transportation Administration ("UMTA"), for grants in the amount
of not less than 75 percent of the cost of constructing the
extension in San Mateo County. SamTrans is required to pay 25
percent of the project cost (the local share) from SamTrans or
other funds. In addition, as part of the agreement, SamTrans is
required to pay BART a total of $200 million as a capital
contribution to the basic BART system. This contribution is
payable in installments which are tied to the progress of the
completion of the extension in San Mateo County. SamTrans has
already paid BART $10 million towards that capital contribution
and will shortly become obligated to pay an additional $90
million to BART.

     On advice of Counsel I refrained from participating in

CFPPC-Request for Advice/JG                    -2-


or influencing the negotiation of the BART-SamTrans Agreement.
Moreover, when the Agreement came before the BART Board for
approval I disclosed my conflict and abstained from voting on it.

     BART is currently in the process of preparing plans and
specifications for contracts to extend the BART system, including
the extension into San Mateo County. Most of these contracts
will be awarded pursuant to competitive bidding laws applicable
to BART. In accordance with the Comprehensive Agreement between
BART and SamTrans, the contracts will be funded from Federal
funds and a local match provided by SamTrans. A recent example
of the funding arrangement is set forth in the attached Executive
Decision Document for a professional services contract which was
prepared for presentation to the BART Board of Directors at a
Board meeting held February 20, 1992. Since SamTrans is to fund
a portion of that contract and on the advice of Counsel, I again
disclosed my business relationship with SamTrans and abstained
from voting on the matter.

     Because the issue of SamTrans funding will continue to
arise as BART constructs the extension in San Mateo County, I am
asking for the advice of the FPPC on whether I am, indeed,
required to abstain from voting on such contracts. I will
emphasize that although SamTrans will provide part of the funding
for the project, the contracts themselves will be between BART
and individual contractors; SamTrans will not be a party to
them. Moreover, the fundamental financial decision which affects
SamTrans, has already been made as a result of the approval and
execution of the Comprehensive Agreement between BART and
SamTrans regarding the extension in San Mateo County. It seems
to me that any effect on SamTrans as a result of the construction
and other contracts related to building the extension will add no
new financial impact but will merely effectuate the existing
financial arrangement between BART and SamTrans. I request your
advice on whether I am permitted to vote on contracts for the
construction of the extension of the BART system in San Mateo
County.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        John Glenn

Attachment




FPPC-ltr

sections.question: I am asking for the advice of the FPPC on whether I am, indeed, required to abstain from voting on such contracts. I will emphasize that although SamTrans will provide part of the funding for the project, the contracts themselves will be between BART and individual contractors; SamTrans will not be a party to them. Moreover, the fundamental financial decision which affects SamTrans, has already been made as a result of the approval and execution of the Comprehensive Agreement between BART and SamTrans regarding the extension in San Mateo County. It seems to me that any effect on SamTrans as a result of the construction and other contracts related to building the extension will add no new financial impact but will merely effectuate the existing financial arrangement between BART and SamTrans. I request your advice on whether I am permitted to vote on contracts for the construction of the extension of the BART system in San Mateo County.
sections.question_synthetic: Must a BART board member abstain from voting on BART contracts for the San Mateo County extension when the board member's insurance adjusting business has received over $250 in annual income from SamTrans, which is funding a portion of the project costs?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response letter. No verbatim CONCLUSION section exists. The question section is reconstructed from the body of the letter as no formal QUESTION header is present. Document shows the requestor has already voluntarily abstained from prior related votes and seeks clarification on ongoing obligation.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: I am asking for the advice of the FPPC on whether I am, indeed, required to abstain from voting on such contracts. I will emphasize that although SamTrans will provide part of the funding for the project, the contracts themselves will be between BART and individual contractors; SamTrans will not be a party to them. Moreover, the fundamental financial decision which affects SamTrans, has already been made as a result of the approval and execution of the Comprehensive Agreement between BART and SamTrans regarding the extension in San Mateo County. It seems to me that any effect on SamTrans as a result of the construction and other contracts related to building the extension will add no new financial impact but will merely effectuate the existing financial arrangement between BART and SamTrans. I request your advice on whether I am permitted to vote on contracts for the construction of the extension of the BART system in San Mateo County.

embedding.summary: John Glenn, an elected BART board member whose insurance adjusting business has received over $250 annually from SamTrans, requests FPPC advice on whether he must abstain from voting on BART contracts for the San Mateo County system extension where SamTrans will provide partial funding.
